rules.rationale: HIPAA applies to covered entities: healthcare providers, health plans, and healthcare clearinghouses
Health technology companies are typically business associates under HIPAA when handling PHI
Health plans are covered entities under HIPAA
CCPA applies to for-profit businesses in California with $25M+ revenue, 50K+ consumers, or 50%+ revenue from selling data
Technology companies often meet CCPA thresholds for consumer data processing
Retail businesses in California commonly collect consumer personal information
Marketing firms typically process significant California consumer data
SOX applies to all publicly traded companies and their auditors
Public technology companies must comply with SOX Section 404
Public accounting firms auditing public companies must comply with SOX
FDA premarket cybersecurity guidance applies to all manufacturers submitting premarket submissions for cyber devices under FD&C Act Section 524B
Technology companies manufacturing software as a medical device (SaMD) or connected medical devices must comply with FDA premarket cybersecurity requirements
FDA 21 CFR Part 11 applies to medical device manufacturers maintaining electronic records for FDA-regulated activities
Medical device manufacturers are business associates under HIPAA when their devices process, store, or transmit PHI
HITECH breach notification requirements apply to medical device manufacturers handling PHI as business associates
FDA postmarket cybersecurity guidance applies to all manufacturers of marketed medical devices containing software or programmable logic
Technology companies manufacturing SaMD or connected medical devices must manage postmarket cybersecurity per FDA guidance
FD&C Act Section 524B (PATCH Act) requires all cyber device manufacturers to submit cybersecurity plans, SBOM, and demonstrate cybersecure design in premarket submissions
Technology companies developing software as a medical device (SaMD) or connected medical devices are subject to Section 524B cyber device requirements
FDA premarket cybersecurity guidance applies to all cyber device manufacturers submitting 510(k), PMA, or De Novo premarket submissions
FDA postmarket cybersecurity guidance applies to all manufacturers of marketed medical devices containing software or programmable logic
FD&C Act Section 524B (PATCH Act) requires all cyber device manufacturers to submit cybersecurity plans, SBOM, and demonstrate cybersecure design
FDA 21 CFR Part 11 applies to medical device manufacturers maintaining electronic records for quality system and design history files
Medical device manufacturers are business associates under HIPAA when their devices process, store, or transmit protected health information
HITECH breach notification requirements apply to medical device manufacturers handling PHI as business associates
Medical device companies with California operations may be subject to CCPA if they meet the revenue or data volume thresholds
21 CFR 820 (Quality System Regulation / QMSR) applies to all manufacturers of finished medical devices intended for human use, including design controls, CAPA, and software validation
Medical device manufacturers in the healthcare sector must comply with 21 CFR 820 quality system requirements including design controls and CAPA
Technology companies manufacturing SaMD or connected medical devices must maintain a 21 CFR 820 quality system
Healthcare and Public Health is one of the 16 critical infrastructure sectors under PPD-21; healthcare entities meeting CISA's size thresholds will be covered entities under CIRCIA

Medical device manufacturers in the Healthcare and Public Health critical infrastructure sector may be covered entities under CIRCIA, especially those whose devices support critical healthcare operations
Financial Services is one of the 16 critical infrastructure sectors under PPD-21; financial institutions meeting CISA's size thresholds will be covered entities under CIRCIA
Information Technology is a critical infrastructure sector under PPD-21; technology companies meeting CISA's thresholds may be covered entities, though the final rule will determine specific criteria
Medical device companies operating in Virginia that process personal data of 100,000+ Virginia consumers or derive 50%+ revenue from selling data of 25,000+ consumers may be subject to VCDPA
Medical device companies doing business in Colorado that control or process personal data of 100,000+ Colorado residents may be subject to the Colorado Privacy Act
Medical device companies operating in Connecticut that process personal data of 100,000+ Connecticut consumers may be subject to CTDPA
